segments.text: In the eighth month of the second year of Darius, the word of the Lord came to the prophet Zechariah son of Berekiah, the son of Iddo:
The Lord was very angry with your ancestors.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Therefore tell the people:
This is what the Lord Almighty says: 'Return to me,' declares the Lord Almighty, 'and I will return to you,'
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: Do not be like your ancestors, to whom the earlier prophets proclaimed:
This is what the Lord Almighty says: 'Turn from your evil ways and your evil practices.'
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: But they would not listen or pay attention to me,
declares the Lord.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Where are your ancestors now? And the prophets, do they live forever?
But did not my words and my decrees, which I commanded my servants the prophets, overtake your ancestors?
Then they repented and said, 'The Lord Almighty has done to us what our ways and practices deserve, just as he determined to do.'
segments.speaker: Ancestors

verses.title: The Man Among the Myrtle Trees

segments.text: On the twenty-fourth day of the eleventh month, the month of Shebat, in the second year of Darius, the word of the Lord came to the prophet Zechariah son of Berekiah, the son of Iddo.
During the night I had a vision, and there before me was a man mounted on a red horse. He was standing among the myrtle trees in a ravine. Behind him were red, brown and white horses.
I asked,
What are these, my lord?
segments.speaker: Zechariah

segments.text: The angel who was talking with me answered,
I will show you what they are.
segments.speaker: Angel

segments.text: Then the man standing among the myrtle trees explained,
They are the ones the Lord has sent to go throughout the earth.
segments.speaker: Man Among Myrtle Trees

segments.text: And they reported to the angel of the Lord who was standing among the myrtle trees,
We have gone throughout the earth and found the whole world at rest and in peace.
segments.speaker: Horsemen

segments.text: Then the angel of the Lord said,
Lord Almighty, how long will you withhold mercy from Jerusalem and from the towns of Judah, which you have been angry with these seventy years?
segments.speaker: Angel of the Lord

segments.text: So the Lord spoke kind and comforting words to the angel who talked with me.
Then the angel who was speaking to me said,
Proclaim this word: This is what the Lord Almighty says: 'I am very jealous for Jerusalem and Zion,
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: and I am very angry with the nations that feel secure. I was only a little angry, but they went too far with the punishment.'
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: Therefore this is what the Lord says: 'I will return to Jerusalem with mercy, and there my house will be rebuilt. And the measuring line will be stretched out over Jerusalem,'
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: declares the Lord Almighty.
Proclaim further: This is what the Lord Almighty says: 'My towns will again overflow with prosperity, and the Lord will again comfort Zion and choose Jerusalem.'
segments.speaker: The Lord Almighty

segments.text: Then I looked up, and there before me were four horns.
I asked the angel who was speaking to me,
What are these?
segments.speaker: Zechariah

segments.text: He answered me,
These are the horns that scattered Judah, Israel and Jerusalem.
segments.speaker: Angel

segments.text: Then the Lord showed me four craftsmen.
I asked,
What are these coming to do?
segments.speaker: Zechariah

segments.text: He answered,

These are the horns that scattered Judah so that no one could raise their head, but the craftsmen have come to terrify them and throw down these horns of the nations who lifted up their horns against the land of Judah to scatter its people.
segments.speaker: Angel
